title: Two Sum
statement: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return the indices of the two numbers that add up to target. Exactly one solution exists and you may not use the same element twice.
constraints: 2 <= len(nums) <= 10^4 | -10^9 <= nums[i] <= 10^9 | Exactly one valid answer.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
statement: You are given prices where prices[i] is the price of a stock on day i. Maximize profit by choosing a day to buy and a later day to sell. Return the max profit, or 0 if no profit is possible.

title: Maximum Subarray
statement: Given an integer array nums, find the contiguous subarray with the largest sum and return that sum (Kadane's algorithm).

title: Move Zeroes
statement: Given an integer array nums, move all 0s to the end while maintaining the relative order of the non-zero elements. Return the resulting array.

title: Product of Array Except Self
statement: Given an integer array nums, return an array answer such that answer[i] equals the product of all elements of nums except nums[i]. Do it without using division.
constraints: 2 <= len(nums) <= 10^5 | The product of any prefix or suffix fits a 32-bit integer.

title: Contains Duplicate
statement: Given an integer array nums, return True if any value appears at least twice, and False if every element is distinct.
solution:
def contains_duplicate(nums):
    return len(set(nums))!=len(nums)

title: Majority Element
statement: Given an array nums of size n, return the majority element (the element that appears more than n/2 times). You may assume it always exists.

title: Rotate Array
statement: Given an integer array nums, rotate the array to the right by k steps, where k is non-negative. Return the rotated array.

title: Merge Intervals
statement: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [start, end], merge all overlapping intervals and return the non-overlapping intervals sorted by start.

title: Valid Anagram
statement: Given two strings s and t, return True if t is an anagram of s, and False otherwise. An anagram uses all original letters exactly once.

title: Valid Palindrome
statement: Given a string s, return True if it is a palindrome considering only alphanumeric characters and ignoring case.

examples.input: s = 'A man, a plan, a canal: Panama'

input: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
statement: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

title: Group Anagrams
statement: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together. Return the groups (order of groups and within groups does not matter).

title: Valid Parentheses
statement: Given a string s containing just the characters '()[]{}', determine if the input string is valid. Brackets must close in the correct order.

title: Reverse String
statement: Write a function that reverses a string, given as an array of characters. Return the reversed array.

title: Two Sum II - Sorted Input
statement: Given a 1-indexed sorted array numbers and a target, return the 1-based indices of the two numbers that add up to target.
constraints: 2 <= len(numbers) <= 3*10^4 | numbers is sorted ascending | exactly one solution
solution:
def two_sum_sorted(numbers, target):
    i,j=0,len(numbers)-1
    while i<j:
        s=numbers[i]+numbers[j]
        if s==target: return [i+1,j+1]
        if s<target: i+=1
        else: j-=1

title: 3Sum
statement: Given an integer array nums, return all unique triplets [a,b,c] such that a+b+c == 0. The solution set must not contain duplicate triplets.
solution:
def three_sum(nums):
    nums=sorted(nums); res=[]; n=len(nums)
    for i in range(n):
        if i>0 and nums[i]==nums[i-1]: continue
        l,r=i+1,n-1
        while l<r:
            s=nums[i]+nums[l]+nums[r]
            if s<0: l+=1
            elif s>0: r-=1
            else:
                res.append([nums[i],nums[l],nums[r]])
                l+=1; r-=1
                while l<r and nums[l]==nums[l-1]: l+=1
                while l<r and nums[r]==nums[r+1]: r-=1
    return res

title: Container With Most Water
statement: Given an integer array height of n non-negative numbers, find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container holding the most water. Return the max area.
solution:
def max_area(height):
    i,j=0,len(height)-1; best=0
    while i<j:
        best=max(best,(j-i)*min(height[i],height[j]))
        if height[i]<height[j]: i+=1
        else: j-=1
    return best

title: Binary Search
statement: Given a sorted array of integers nums and a target, return the index of target if it exists, otherwise return -1.
constraints: 1 <= len(nums) <= 10^4 | nums sorted ascending, all distinct
solution:
def search(nums, target):
    lo,hi=0,len(nums)-1
    while lo<=hi:
        m=(lo+hi)//2
        if nums[m]==target: return m
        if nums[m]<target: lo=m+1
        else: hi=m-1
    return -1

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
statement: An ascending sorted array was rotated at an unknown pivot. Given the array nums and a target, return its index, or -1 if not present. Run in O(log n).

title: Sqrt(x)
statement: Given a non-negative integer x, return the integer square root of x (the floor of the real square root). Do not use built-in sqrt.
solution:
def my_sqrt(x):
    lo,hi=0,x; ans=0
    while lo<=hi:
        m=(lo+hi)//2
        if m*m<=x: ans=m; lo=m+1
        else: hi=m-1
    return ans

title: Subarray Sum Equals K
statement: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the total number of contiguous subarrays whose sum equals k.

title: Top K Frequent Elements
statement: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the k most frequent elements. The answer may be returned in any order.
constraints: 1 <= len(nums) <= 10^5 | k is in range [1, number of unique elements]

title: Daily Temperatures
statement: Given an array temperatures, return an array answer where answer[i] is the number of days you have to wait after day i to get a warmer temperature. If there is no future warmer day, answer[i] = 0.

title: Sort Colors
statement: Given an array nums with n objects colored 0, 1, or 2, sort them in-place so objects of the same color are adjacent in the order 0,1,2 (Dutch National Flag). Return the array.
solution:
def sort_colors(nums):
    lo,mid,hi=0,0,len(nums)-1
    while mid<=hi:
        if nums[mid]==0: nums[lo],nums[mid]=nums[mid],nums[lo]; lo+=1; mid+=1
        elif nums[mid]==1: mid+=1
        else: nums[mid],nums[hi]=nums[hi],nums[mid]; hi-=1
    return nums

title: Merge Sort
statement: Implement merge sort. Given an array nums, return a new sorted array in ascending order using the divide-and-conquer merge sort algorithm.
solution:
def merge_sort(nums):
    if len(nums)<=1: return nums[:]
    mid=len(nums)//2
    L=merge_sort(nums[:mid]); R=merge_sort(nums[mid:])
    out=[]; i=j=0
    while i<len(L) and j<len(R):
        if L[i]<=R[j]: out.append(L[i]); i+=1
        else: out.append(R[j]); j+=1
    out.extend(L[i:]); out.extend(R[j:])
    return out

title: Fibonacci Number
statement: The Fibonacci numbers form a sequence where F(0)=0, F(1)=1, and F(n)=F(n-1)+F(n-2). Given n, return F(n).
solution:
def fib(n):
    a,b=0,1
    for _ in range(n): a,b=b,a+b
    return a

title: Pow(x, n)
statement: Implement pow(x, n), which calculates x raised to the integer power n, using fast exponentiation. Return the result rounded is not needed; floating answer is fine within 1e-5.
solution:
def my_pow(x, n):
    if n<0: x=1/x; n=-n
    res=1.0
    while n:
        if n&1: res*=x
        x*=x; n>>=1
    return res

title: Climbing Stairs
statement: You are climbing a staircase that takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can climb 1 or 2 steps. Return the number of distinct ways to reach the top.
solution:
def climb_stairs(n):
    a,b=1,1
    for _ in range(n): a,b=b,a+b
    return a

title: House Robber
statement: Given an integer array nums representing money in each house, return the maximum amount you can rob without robbing two adjacent houses.

title: Coin Change
statement: Given coins of different denominations and a total amount, return the fewest number of coins needed to make up that amount. If it cannot be made, return -1.

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
statement: Given an integer array nums, return the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence.

title: Unique Paths
statement: A robot is at the top-left of an m x n grid and can only move right or down. Return the number of unique paths to reach the bottom-right corner.

title: Jump Game
statement: Given an integer array nums where each element is the maximum jump length at that position, return True if you can reach the last index starting from index 0.

title: Gas Station
statement: There are n gas stations on a circular route. gas[i] is the fuel at station i, cost[i] is the fuel needed to travel to the next. Return the starting index from which you can complete the circuit once, or -1 if impossible.
solution:
def can_complete_circuit(gas, cost):
    if sum(gas)<sum(cost): return -1
    tank=0; start=0
    for i in range(len(gas)):
        tank+=gas[i]-cost[i]
        if tank<0: start=i+1; tank=0
    return start

title: Fizz Buzz
statement: Given an integer n, return a string array answer (1-indexed) where answer[i] is 'FizzBuzz' if i is divisible by 3 and 5, 'Fizz' if divisible by 3, 'Buzz' if divisible by 5, otherwise the number as a string.

title: Reverse Integer
statement: Given a signed 32-bit integer x, return x with its digits reversed. If reversing causes the value to go outside the 32-bit signed integer range [-2^31, 2^31-1], return 0.
solution:
def reverse(x):
    sign=-1 if x<0 else 1
    r=int(str(abs(x))[::-1])*sign
    return r if -2**31<=r<=2**31-1 else 0

title: Palindrome Number
statement: Given an integer x, return True if x is a palindrome (reads the same backward as forward), and False otherwise. Negative numbers are not palindromes.

title: Roman to Integer
statement: Given a Roman numeral string s, convert it to an integer. Symbols: I=1, V=5, X=10, L=50, C=100, D=500, M=1000. Subtractive pairs like IV=4 and IX=9 apply.
constraints: 1 <= len(s) <= 15 | valid roman numeral in range [1,3999]

title: Running Sum of 1D Array
statement: Given an array nums, return its running (prefix) sum where result[i] = nums[0] + nums[1] + ... + nums[i].

title: Find Minimum and Maximum
statement: Return a list [minimum, maximum] of the array in a single pass.

title: Second Largest Element
statement: Return the second largest distinct element in the array, or -1 if it does not exist.

title: Max Consecutive Ones
statement: Given a binary array nums, return the maximum number of consecutive 1s.

title: Missing Number
statement: nums contains n distinct numbers in the range [0, n]. Return the only number in the range that is missing.
constraints: n == len(nums) | 0 <= nums[i] <= n | all distinct

title: Single Number
statement: Every element appears twice except for one. Find that single one using O(1) extra space.
constraints: Each element appears twice except one.
solution:
def single_number(nums):
    r=0
    for x in nums: r^=x
    return r

title: Find All Numbers Disappeared
statement: Given nums of n integers where nums[i] is in [1, n], return all integers in [1, n] that do not appear in nums.

title: Maximum Product Subarray
statement: Find the contiguous subarray with the largest product and return that product.
solution:
def max_product(nums):
    best=cur_max=cur_min=nums[0]
    for x in nums[1:]:
        cands=(x, cur_max*x, cur_min*x)
        cur_max=max(cands); cur_min=min(cands)
        best=max(best,cur_max)
    return best

title: Count Pairs With Given Sum
statement: Count the number of index pairs (i, j) with i < j such that nums[i] + nums[j] == target.

title: Equilibrium Index
statement: Return the leftmost index i where the sum of elements to the left equals the sum to the right, or -1 if none exists.

title: Trapping Rain Water
statement: Given non-negative integers height representing an elevation map where the width of each bar is 1, compute how much water it can trap after raining.

title: Majority Element II
statement: Return all elements that appear more than ⌊n/3⌋ times, in any order.
solution:
def majority_three(nums):
    from collections import Counter
    n=len(nums)
    return [k for k,v in Counter(nums).items() if v>n//3]

title: Leaders in an Array
statement: An element is a leader if it is greater than or equal to all elements to its right. Return all leaders in their original order (the rightmost element is always a leader).

title: Wave Array
statement: Sort the array into a wave-like form: nums[0] >= nums[1] <= nums[2] >= nums[3] ... Return the array after sorting ascending then swapping adjacent pairs.

title: Maximum Circular Subarray Sum
statement: Return the maximum possible sum of a non-empty subarray of a circular array nums (the end connects to the beginning).

title: Count Inversions
statement: Count the number of inversions: pairs (i, j) with i < j and nums[i] > nums[j].
solution:
def count_inversions(nums):
    def sort(a):
        if len(a)<=1: return a,0
        m=len(a)//2
        L,x=sort(a[:m]); R,y=sort(a[m:])
        merged=[]; i=j=0; inv=x+y
        while i<len(L) and j<len(R):
            if L[i]<=R[j]: merged.append(L[i]); i+=1
            else: merged.append(R[j]); j+=1; inv+=len(L)-i
        merged+=L[i:]+R[j:]
        return merged,inv
    return sort(nums)[1]

title: First Missing Positive
statement: Given an unsorted integer array nums, return the smallest positive integer that does not appear in it.
solution:
def first_missing_positive(nums):
    s=set(nums); i=1
    while i in s: i+=1
    return i

title: Subarray Product Less Than K
statement: Count the number of contiguous subarrays where the product of all elements is strictly less than k.
solution:
def num_subarray_product(nums, k):
    if k<=1: return 0
    prod=1; left=0; res=0
    for right,x in enumerate(nums):
        prod*=x
        while prod>=k: prod//=nums[left]; left+=1
        res+=right-left+1
    return res

title: Maximum Average Subarray I
statement: Find a contiguous subarray of length exactly k that has the maximum average, and return that average value.

title: Find the Duplicate Number
statement: Given nums of n+1 integers where each integer is in [1, n], exactly one number is repeated. Return the duplicate.
constraints: There is exactly one repeated number.
solution:
def find_duplicate(nums):
    slow=fast=nums[0]
    while True:
        slow=nums[slow]; fast=nums[nums[fast]]
        if slow==fast: break
    slow=nums[0]
    while slow!=fast: slow=nums[slow]; fast=nums[fast]
    return slow

title: Sort Array By Parity
statement: Move all even-valued elements before all odd-valued ones, preserving the original relative order within evens and within odds.
solution:
def sort_by_parity(nums):
    return [x for x in nums if x%2==0]+[x for x in nums if x%2==1]

title: Max Chunks To Make Sorted
statement: arr is a permutation of [0, n-1]. Split it into the maximum number of chunks so that sorting each chunk individually and concatenating yields the fully sorted array. Return the number of chunks.
constraints: arr is a permutation of [0, n-1]

title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
statement: Given an unsorted array nums, return the length of the longest run of consecutive integers (in O(n) on average).

title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
statement: Given an array of positive integers nums and a positive integer target, return the minimal length of a contiguous subarray whose sum is >= target. If none exists, return 0.

title: Reverse Words in a String
statement: Given an input string s, reverse the order of the words. A word is a maximal sequence of non-space characters. Return a string of the words in reverse order joined by a single space, with no leading/trailing spaces.
solution:
def reverse_words(s):
    return ' '.join(reversed(s.split()))

title: First Unique Character
statement: Given a string s, return the index of the first non-repeating character. If none exists, return -1.

title: Longest Common Prefix
statement: Find the longest common prefix string amongst an array of strings. If there is no common prefix, return an empty string.

title: Is Palindrome (alphanumeric)
statement: Return True if s reads the same forwards and backwards considering only lowercase letters a-z (ignore everything else).

title: Count Vowels and Consonants
statement: Given a lowercase string s, return a list [vowels, consonants] counting the alphabetic characters.
solution:
def count_vc(s):
    v=sum(1 for c in s if c in 'aeiou')
    cons=sum(1 for c in s if c.isalpha() and c not in 'aeiou')
    return [v, cons]

title: String Compression (Run Length)
statement: Compress the string by replacing runs of the same character with the character followed by the run length (counts of 1 are still written). Return the compressed string.

title: Isomorphic Strings
statement: Two strings s and t are isomorphic if the characters in s can be replaced to get t with a consistent one-to-one mapping. Return True or False.

title: Valid Anagram (counts)
statement: Return True if t is an anagram of s (same multiset of characters).

title: Longest Palindromic Substring
statement: Given a string s, return the longest contiguous substring that is a palindrome. If several have the same length, return the one that starts earliest.

title: Group Anagrams
statement: Group the strings that are anagrams of each other. Return the groups in any order.

title: Count Distinct Characters Runs
statement: Return the number of contiguous groups of identical characters. For 'aaabbbcc' there are 3 groups (aaa, bbb, cc).

title: String to Integer (atoi)
statement: Convert a string to a 32-bit signed integer: skip leading spaces, read an optional +/-, then digits until a non-digit. Clamp to [-2^31, 2^31-1]. If no digits are read, return 0.

title: Repeated Substring Pattern
statement: Return True if the string can be constructed by taking a substring of it and appending multiple copies of that substring together.
solution:
def repeated_pattern(s):
    return s in (s+s)[1:-1]

title: Reverse Vowels of a String
statement: Reverse only the vowels (a,e,i,o,u) in the string, leaving other characters in place. Return the result.

title: Check String Rotation
statement: Return True if string b is a rotation of string a (e.g. 'erbottlewat' is a rotation of 'waterbottle').
solution:
def is_rotation(a, b):
    return len(a)==len(b) and b in (a+a)

title: Minimum Window Substring
statement: Given strings s and t, return the smallest substring of s that contains every character of t (including duplicates). If there is no such window, return ''. If there are ties, return the one starting earliest.

title: Word Pattern
statement: Given a pattern of letters and a string s of space-separated words, return True if there is a one-to-one correspondence between each letter and each word.

title: Length of Last Word
statement: Given a string s consisting of words and spaces, return the length of the last word.

title: Excel Sheet Column Number
statement: Given a column title as it appears in an Excel sheet (A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB, ...), return its corresponding column number.

title: Ransom Note
statement: Given two strings ransomNote and magazine, return True if ransomNote can be constructed using the letters from magazine (each letter used at most as many times as it appears).

title: Add Binary
statement: Given two binary strings a and b, return their sum as a binary string.
solution:
def add_binary(a, b):
    return bin(int(a,2)+int(b,2))[2:]

title: Longest Substring with K Distinct
statement: Given a string s and integer k, return the length of the longest substring that contains at most k distinct characters.
solution:
def longest_k_distinct(s, k):
    from collections import defaultdict
    if k==0: return 0
    count=defaultdict(int); left=0; best=0
    for right,ch in enumerate(s):
        count[ch]+=1
        while len(count)>k:
            count[s[left]]-=1
            if count[s[left]]==0: del count[s[left]]
            left+=1
        best=max(best,right-left+1)
    return best

title: Two Sum (Hash Map)
statement: Return indices of the two numbers that add up to target using a single-pass hash map.

title: Subarray Sum Equals K
statement: Return the number of contiguous subarrays whose sum equals k.

title: Intersection of Two Arrays
statement: Return the unique elements present in both arrays, in any order.
solution:
def intersection(nums1, nums2):
    return list(set(nums1) & set(nums2))

title: Happy Number
statement: A number is happy if repeatedly replacing it by the sum of squares of its digits eventually reaches 1. Return True if n is happy.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating
statement: Return the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

title: 4Sum II (Count)
statement: Given four integer arrays a, b, c, d of the same length n, count tuples (i,j,k,l) such that a[i]+b[j]+c[k]+d[l]==0.
starter:
def four_sum_count(a, b, c, d):
    pass

title: Top K Frequent Elements
statement: Return the k most frequent elements in any order.
constraints: k is valid for the input

title: Contains Nearby Duplicate
statement: Return True if there are two distinct indices i and j such that nums[i]==nums[j] and |i-j| <= k.

title: Find All Anagrams in a String
statement: Return the start indices of all substrings of s that are anagrams of p, in increasing order.

title: Subarray Sums Divisible by K
statement: Return the number of contiguous subarrays whose sum is divisible by k.

title: Count Unique Frequencies
statement: Given an array, return True if the number of occurrences of every value is unique (no two values occur the same number of times).

title: Smallest Index With Equal Value
statement: Return the smallest index i such that i mod 10 == nums[i], or -1 if no such index exists.

title: Two Sum II (Sorted)
statement: Given a 1-indexed sorted array and a target, return the 1-based indices of the two numbers adding to target.
constraints: Exactly one solution; array sorted ascending.
solution:
def two_sum_sorted_tp(numbers, target):
    i,j=0,len(numbers)-1
    while i<j:
        s=numbers[i]+numbers[j]
        if s==target: return [i+1,j+1]
        if s<target: i+=1
        else: j-=1

title: 3Sum
statement: Return all unique triplets [a,b,c] from nums such that a+b+c == 0.
solution:
def three_sum_tp(nums):
    nums=sorted(nums); res=[]; n=len(nums)
    for i in range(n):
        if i>0 and nums[i]==nums[i-1]: continue
        l,r=i+1,n-1
        while l<r:
            s=nums[i]+nums[l]+nums[r]
            if s<0: l+=1
            elif s>0: r-=1
            else:
                res.append([nums[i],nums[l],nums[r]]); l+=1; r-=1
                while l<r and nums[l]==nums[l-1]: l+=1
                while l<r and nums[r]==nums[r+1]: r-=1
    return res

title: Container With Most Water
statement: Find two lines that with the x-axis form a container holding the most water; return the area.
solution:
def max_area_tp(height):
    i,j=0,len(height)-1; best=0
    while i<j:
        best=max(best,(j-i)*min(height[i],height[j]))
        if height[i]<height[j]: i+=1
        else: j-=1
    return best

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
statement: Given a sorted array, remove duplicates in place and return the array of unique elements in order.

title: Move Zeroes
statement: Move all 0s to the end while preserving the relative order of non-zero elements. Return the array.

title: Sort Colors (Dutch Flag)
statement: Sort an array of 0s, 1s and 2s in place in a single pass. Return the array.
solution:
def sort_colors_tp(nums):
    lo,mid,hi=0,0,len(nums)-1
    while mid<=hi:
        if nums[mid]==0: nums[lo],nums[mid]=nums[mid],nums[lo]; lo+=1; mid+=1
        elif nums[mid]==1: mid+=1
        else: nums[mid],nums[hi]=nums[hi],nums[mid]; hi-=1
    return nums

title: Is Subsequence
statement: Return True if s is a subsequence of t (s can be formed by deleting some characters of t without reordering).

title: Squares of a Sorted Array
statement: Given a sorted (ascending) integer array nums, return an array of the squares of each number, sorted ascending.
solution:
def sorted_squares(nums):
    n=len(nums); res=[0]*n; l,r=0,n-1; pos=n-1
    while l<=r:
        if abs(nums[l])>abs(nums[r]): res[pos]=nums[l]**2; l+=1
        else: res[pos]=nums[r]**2; r-=1
        pos-=1
    return res

title: 3Sum Closest
statement: Find three integers in nums whose sum is closest to target, and return that sum.
solution:
def three_sum_closest(nums, target):
    nums=sorted(nums); best=nums[0]+nums[1]+nums[2]; n=len(nums)
    for i in range(n):
        l,r=i+1,n-1
        while l<r:
            s=nums[i]+nums[l]+nums[r]
            if abs(s-target)<abs(best-target): best=s
            if s<target: l+=1
            elif s>target: r-=1
            else: return s
    return best

title: Backspace String Compare
statement: Given two strings where '#' means a backspace, return True if they are equal after applying backspaces.

title: Partition Array Around Pivot Count
statement: Given nums and a pivot value, return a list [less, equal, greater] counting elements strictly less than, equal to, and greater than pivot.
solution:
def partition_counts(nums, pivot):
    less=sum(1 for x in nums if x<pivot)
    eq=sum(1 for x in nums if x==pivot)
    gr=sum(1 for x in nums if x>pivot)
    return [less,eq,gr]

title: Maximum Sum Subarray of Size K
statement: Given an array and integer k, return the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size exactly k.

title: Max Consecutive Ones III
statement: Given a binary array nums and integer k, return the length of the longest subarray of 1s after flipping at most k zeros.

title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
statement: Given a string s and integer k, return the length of the longest substring containing a single repeated letter after replacing at most k characters.

title: Permutation in String
statement: Return True if s2 contains a permutation of s1 as a contiguous substring.
solution:
def check_inclusion(s1, s2):
    from collections import Counter
    k=len(s1)
    if k>len(s2): return False
    need=Counter(s1); window=Counter(s2[:k])
    if window==need: return True
    for i in range(k,len(s2)):
        window[s2[i]]+=1; window[s2[i-k]]-=1
        if window[s2[i-k]]==0: del window[s2[i-k]]
        if window==need: return True
    return False

title: Count Number of Nice Subarrays
statement: Given an array nums and integer k, return the number of contiguous subarrays that contain exactly k odd numbers.

title: Fruit Into Baskets
statement: Given an array fruits where fruits[i] is a fruit type, return the length of the longest subarray that contains at most 2 distinct types.
solution:
def total_fruit(fruits):
    from collections import defaultdict
    count=defaultdict(int); left=0; best=0
    for right,f in enumerate(fruits):
        count[f]+=1
        while len(count)>2:
            count[fruits[left]]-=1
            if count[fruits[left]]==0: del count[fruits[left]]
            left+=1
        best=max(best,right-left+1)
    return best

title: Shortest Subarray With Sum At Least Target (Positive)
statement: Given an array of positive integers nums and a positive target, return the length of the shortest contiguous subarray with sum >= target, or 0 if none.

title: Valid Parentheses
statement: Given a string of '()[]{}', return True if every bracket is closed by the same type in the correct order.

title: Next Greater Element
statement: For each element, find the next element to its right that is strictly greater. If none exists use -1. Return the array of answers.

title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
statement: Evaluate an arithmetic expression in Reverse Polish Notation (tokens are numbers or +, -, *, /). Division truncates toward zero. Return the integer result.

title: Decode String
statement: Decode a string encoded as k[encoded] meaning the bracketed part is repeated k times (nesting allowed). Return the decoded string.

title: Asteroid Collision
statement: Each asteroid moves right (positive) or left (negative) at the same speed. When two collide the smaller explodes; equal sizes both explode. Return the surviving asteroids.

title: Remove All Adjacent Duplicates
statement: Repeatedly remove two adjacent equal characters until none remain. Return the final string.

title: Baseball Game
statement: Process operations on a record: an integer adds that score; '+' adds the sum of the previous two; 'D' adds double the previous; 'C' removes the previous. Return the total sum.

title: Minimum Add to Make Parentheses Valid
statement: Return the minimum number of parentheses to add to make the string valid.

title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
statement: Given heights of bars of width 1, return the area of the largest rectangle that fits within the histogram.

title: Simplify Path
statement: Given an absolute Unix-style path, simplify it to its canonical form ('.' stays, '..' goes up, multiple slashes collapse).

title: Implement Queue using Stacks
statement: Simulate a FIFO queue. Given ops where each is ['push',x], ['pop'], or ['peek'], return the list of outputs produced by 'pop' and 'peek' operations in order.

title: Number of Recent Calls
statement: Given a list of ping timestamps (strictly increasing), for each ping return the number of pings in the inclusive window [t-3000, t]. Return the list of counts.

title: Binary Search
statement: Given a sorted array of distinct integers and a target, return its index or -1 if absent. O(log n).
solution:
def bsearch(nums, target):
    lo,hi=0,len(nums)-1
    while lo<=hi:
        m=(lo+hi)//2
        if nums[m]==target: return m
        if nums[m]<target: lo=m+1
        else: hi=m-1
    return -1

title: Search Insert Position
statement: Return the index where target is found, or the index where it would be inserted to keep the array sorted.
solution:
def search_insert(nums, target):
    lo,hi=0,len(nums)
    while lo<hi:
        m=(lo+hi)//2
        if nums[m]<target: lo=m+1
        else: hi=m
    return lo

title: Find First and Last Position
statement: Given a sorted array and a target, return [first index, last index] of the target, or [-1,-1] if not found.
solution:
def search_range(nums, target):
    import bisect
    lo=bisect.bisect_left(nums,target)
    hi=bisect.bisect_right(nums,target)-1
    if lo<=hi and lo<len(nums) and nums[lo]==target: return [lo,hi]
    return [-1,-1]

title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
statement: A sorted array of distinct values was rotated. Return its minimum element in O(log n).
solution:
def find_min(nums):
    lo,hi=0,len(nums)-1
    while lo<hi:
        m=(lo+hi)//2
        if nums[m]>nums[hi]: lo=m+1
        else: hi=m
    return nums[lo]

title: Peak Index in a Mountain Array
statement: A mountain array strictly increases then strictly decreases. Return the index of the peak element.
solution:
def peak_index(arr):
    lo,hi=0,len(arr)-1
    while lo<hi:
        m=(lo+hi)//2
        if arr[m]<arr[m+1]: lo=m+1
        else: hi=m
    return lo

title: Koko Eating Bananas
statement: Koko eats bananas from piles; with speed k she eats min(k, pile) per hour. Return the minimum integer speed k to finish all piles within h hours.
solution:
def min_eating_speed(piles, h):
    import math
    lo,hi=1,max(piles)
    while lo<hi:
        m=(lo+hi)//2
        hours=sum(math.ceil(p/m) for p in piles)
        if hours<=h: hi=m
        else: lo=m+1
    return lo

title: Single Element in a Sorted Array
statement: In a sorted array where every element appears exactly twice except one, return the single element. O(log n).
solution:
def single_non_duplicate(nums):
    lo,hi=0,len(nums)-1
    while lo<hi:
        m=(lo+hi)//2
        if m%2==1: m-=1
        if nums[m]==nums[m+1]: lo=m+2
        else: hi=m
    return nums[lo]

title: Search a 2D Matrix
statement: Given an m x n matrix where each row is sorted ascending and the first integer of each row is greater than the last of the previous row, return True if target exists.
solution:
def search_matrix(matrix, target):
    if not matrix or not matrix[0]: return False
    m,n=len(matrix),len(matrix[0])
    lo,hi=0,m*n-1
    while lo<=hi:
        mid=(lo+hi)//2; v=matrix[mid//n][mid%n]
        if v==target: return True
        if v<target: lo=mid+1
        else: hi=mid-1
    return False

title: Guess Number Higher or Lower
statement: A number pick is hidden in [1, n]. Given n and the secret pick, return pick (simulate binary search guessing). The expected answer is simply pick.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
statement: Merge two sorted linked lists into one sorted list and return it (as an array of values).
starter:
# Definition for singly-linked list (already available):
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None): self.val, self.next = val, next
def merge_two_lists(l1, l2):
    pass
solution:
def merge_two_lists(l1, l2):
    dummy=cur=ListNode()
    while l1 and l2:
        if l1.val<=l2.val: cur.next=l1; l1=l1.next
        else: cur.next=l2; l2=l2.next
        cur=cur.next
    cur.next=l1 or l2
    return dummy.next

title: Remove Nth Node From End
statement: Remove the n-th node from the end of the list and return the head (as an array).
starter:
# Definition for singly-linked list (already available):
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None): self.val, self.next = val, next
def remove_nth(head, n):
    pass
solution:
def remove_nth(head, n):
    dummy=ListNode(0,head); fast=slow=dummy
    for _ in range(n): fast=fast.next
    while fast.next: fast=fast.next; slow=slow.next
    slow.next=slow.next.next
    return dummy.next

title: Odd Even Linked List
statement: Group all nodes at odd positions together followed by nodes at even positions (1-indexed), preserving order. Return as an array.
starter:
# Definition for singly-linked list (already available):
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None): self.val, self.next = val, next
def odd_even_list(head):
    pass
solution:
def odd_even_list(head):
    if not head: return None
    odd=head; even=head.next; even_head=even
    while even and even.next:
        odd.next=even.next; odd=odd.next
        even.next=odd.next; even=even.next
    odd.next=even_head
    return head

title: Palindrome Linked List
statement: Return True if the linked list values read the same forwards and backwards.
starter:
# Definition for singly-linked list (already available):
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None): self.val, self.next = val, next
def is_palindrome_ll(head):
    pass
solution:
def is_palindrome_ll(head):
    arr=[]
    while head: arr.append(head.val); head=head.next
    return arr==arr[::-1]

title: Add Two Numbers
statement: Two non-empty linked lists represent two non-negative integers with digits stored in reverse order. Add the numbers and return the sum as a linked list (reverse order).
constraints: Each list represents a number without leading zeros (except 0 itself).
starter:
# Definition for singly-linked list (already available):
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None): self.val, self.next = val, next
def add_two_numbers(l1, l2):
    pass
solution:
def add_two_numbers(l1, l2):
    dummy=cur=ListNode(); carry=0
    while l1 or l2 or carry:
        s=carry+(l1.val if l1 else 0)+(l2.val if l2 else 0)
        carry=s//10; cur.next=ListNode(s%10); cur=cur.next
        l1=l1.next if l1 else None; l2=l2.next if l2 else None
    return dummy.next

title: Rotate List
statement: Rotate the linked list to the right by k places. Return as an array.
starter:
# Definition for singly-linked list (already available):
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None): self.val, self.next = val, next
def rotate_right(head, k):
    pass
solution:
def rotate_right(head, k):
    if not head or not head.next: return head
    n=1; tail=head
    while tail.next: tail=tail.next; n+=1
    k%=n
    if k==0: return head
    tail.next=head
    steps=n-k; new_tail=head
    for _ in range(steps-1): new_tail=new_tail.next
    new_head=new_tail.next; new_tail.next=None
    return new_head

title: Reverse Linked List II
statement: Reverse the nodes of the list from position left to position right (1-indexed) and return the list as an array.
starter:
# Definition for singly-linked list (already available):
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None): self.val, self.next = val, next
def reverse_between(head, left, right):
    pass
solution:
def reverse_between(head, left, right):
    dummy=ListNode(0,head); prev=dummy
    for _ in range(left-1): prev=prev.next
    cur=prev.next
    for _ in range(right-left):
        nxt=cur.next; cur.next=nxt.next; nxt.next=prev.next; prev.next=nxt
    return dummy.next

title: Partition List
statement: Partition the list so that all nodes less than x come before nodes greater than or equal to x, preserving the original relative order in each part. Return as an array.
starter:
# Definition for singly-linked list (already available):
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None): self.val, self.next = val, next
def partition_list(head, x):
    pass
solution:
def partition_list(head, x):
    less=lh=ListNode(); ge=gh=ListNode()
    while head:
        if head.val<x: less.next=head; less=less.next
        else: ge.next=head; ge=ge.next
        head=head.next
    ge.next=None; less.next=gh.next
    return lh.next

title: Convert Binary Linked List to Integer
statement: The linked list holds a binary number (each node is 0 or 1, most-significant bit first). Return its decimal value.
starter:
# Definition for singly-linked list (already available):
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None): self.val, self.next = val, next
def get_decimal(head):
    pass
solution:
def get_decimal(head):
    n=0
    while head: n=n*2+head.val; head=head.next
    return n

title: Delete the Middle Node
statement: Delete the middle node of the list (for even length, the second of the two middles) and return the list as an array.
starter:
# Definition for singly-linked list (already available):
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None): self.val, self.next = val, next
def delete_middle(head):
    pass
solution:
def delete_middle(head):
    if not head.next: return None
    dummy=ListNode(0,head); slow=dummy; fast=head
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow=slow.next; fast=fast.next.next
    slow.next=slow.next.next
    return head

title: Invert Binary Tree
statement: Invert (mirror) the binary tree and return it as a level-order array.
starter:
# Definition for a binary tree node (already available):
# class TreeNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None): self.val, self.left, self.right = val, left, right
def invert_tree(root):
    pass
solution:
def invert_tree(root):
    if not root: return None
    root.left, root.right = invert_tree(root.right), invert_tree(root.left)
    return root

title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
statement: Return the k-th smallest value (1-indexed) in a binary search tree.
starter:
# Definition for a binary tree node (already available):
# class TreeNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None): self.val, self.left, self.right = val, left, right
def kth_smallest(root, k):
    pass
solution:
def kth_smallest(root, k):
    st=[]; cur=root
    while st or cur:
        while cur: st.append(cur); cur=cur.left
        cur=st.pop(); k-=1
        if k==0: return cur.val
        cur=cur.right

title: Merge Two Binary Trees
statement: Merge two binary trees by adding overlapping node values; if only one node exists, use it. Return the merged tree as a level-order array.
starter:
# Definition for a binary tree node (already available):
# class TreeNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None): self.val, self.left, self.right = val, left, right
def merge_trees(t1, t2):
    pass
solution:
def merge_trees(t1, t2):
    if not t1: return t2
    if not t2: return t1
    t1.val+=t2.val
    t1.left=merge_trees(t1.left,t2.left)
    t1.right=merge_trees(t1.right,t2.right)
    return t1

title: Climbing Stairs
statement: You can climb 1 or 2 steps at a time. Return the number of distinct ways to reach the top of n steps.
solution:
def climb_stairs(n):
    a,b=1,1
    for _ in range(n): a,b=b,a+b
    return a

title: Decode Ways
statement: A message of digits is encoded with A=1..Z=26. Return the number of ways to decode the string s.
solution:
def num_decodings(s):
    if not s or s[0]=='0': return 0
    prev,cur=1,1
    for i in range(1,len(s)):
        tmp=0
        if s[i]!='0': tmp+=cur
        if 10<=int(s[i-1:i+1])<=26: tmp+=prev
        prev,cur=cur,tmp
    return cur

title: Gas Station
statement: Return the starting station index from which you can travel the circular route once, or -1 if impossible.
solution:
def gas_station(gas, cost):
    if sum(gas)<sum(cost): return -1
    tank=0; start=0
    for i in range(len(gas)):
        tank+=gas[i]-cost[i]
        if tank<0: start=i+1; tank=0
    return start

title: Assign Cookies
statement: Each child i has greed g[i]; each cookie j has size s[j]. A child is content if assigned a cookie with size >= greed. Return the maximum number of content children.

title: Non-overlapping Intervals
statement: Return the minimum number of intervals to remove so the rest are non-overlapping.

title: Subsets
statement: Return all possible subsets (the power set) of an array of distinct integers, in any order.

title: Permutations
statement: Return all possible permutations of an array of distinct integers, in any order.

title: Combination Sum
statement: Given distinct candidates and a target, return all unique combinations where the chosen numbers (each usable unlimited times) sum to target.

title: Generate Parentheses
statement: Given n pairs of parentheses, return all combinations of well-formed parentheses, in any order.

title: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
statement: Given digits 2-9, return all letter combinations the number could represent (phone keypad mapping), in any order. Empty input returns [].

title: Combinations
statement: Return all possible combinations of k numbers chosen from the range [1, n], in any order.

title: Palindrome Partitioning
statement: Return all ways to partition string s so that every substring of the partition is a palindrome, in any order.

title: Number of 1 Bits
statement: Return the number of set bits (1s) in the binary representation of a non-negative integer n.
solution:
def hamming_weight(n):
    c=0
    while n: n&=n-1; c+=1
    return c

title: Reverse Bits
statement: Reverse the bits of a 32-bit unsigned integer and return the resulting integer.
solution:
def reverse_bits(n):
    res=0
    for _ in range(32): res=(res<<1)|(n&1); n>>=1
    return res

title: Counting Bits
statement: Given n, return an array of length n+1 where ans[i] is the number of 1s in the binary representation of i.

title: Single Number (XOR)
statement: Every element appears twice except one. Return the unique element.
solution:
def single_xor(nums):
    r=0
    for x in nums: r^=x
    return r

title: Power of Two
statement: Return True if n is a power of two.
solution:
def is_power_of_two(n):
    return n>0 and (n & (n-1))==0

title: Missing Number (XOR)
statement: nums has n distinct numbers from [0, n]. Return the missing one using XOR.
solution:
def missing_xor(nums):
    r=len(nums)
    for i,x in enumerate(nums): r^=i^x
    return r

title: Greatest Common Divisor
statement: Return the greatest common divisor of two positive integers using the Euclidean algorithm.

title: Count Primes
statement: Return the number of prime numbers strictly less than n.

title: Add Digits (Digital Root)
statement: Repeatedly add all the digits of n until the result has a single digit, and return it.
solution:
def add_digits(n):
    return 0 if n==0 else 1+(n-1)%9

title: Excel Sheet Column Title
statement: Given a positive integer, return its corresponding Excel column title (1->A, 28->AB, ...).

title: Power of Three
statement: Return True if n is a power of three.
solution:
def is_power_of_three(n):
    if n<1: return False
    while n%3==0: n//=3
    return n==1

title: Integer Square Root
statement: Return the floor of the square root of a non-negative integer x without using built-in sqrt.
solution:
def isqrt(x):
    lo,hi,ans=0,x,0
    while lo<=hi:
        m=(lo+hi)//2
        if m*m<=x: ans=m; lo=m+1
        else: hi=m-1
    return ans

title: Ugly Number
statement: An ugly number's only prime factors are 2, 3 and 5. Return True if n is ugly (1 is ugly).

title: Plus One
statement: Given a non-negative integer represented as an array of digits (most significant first), add one and return the resulting digit array.

title: Fizz Buzz
statement: Return a 1-indexed string array of length n: 'FizzBuzz' for multiples of 15, 'Fizz' for 3, 'Buzz' for 5, otherwise the number.

title: Transpose Matrix
statement: Return the transpose of the matrix (flip over its main diagonal).
solution:
def transpose(matrix):
    return [list(row) for row in zip(*matrix)]

title: Rotate Image
statement: Rotate an n x n matrix 90 degrees clockwise and return it.
solution:
def rotate_image(matrix):
    return [list(row) for row in zip(*matrix[::-1])]

title: Spiral Matrix
statement: Return all elements of the matrix in spiral order (clockwise starting top-left).

title: Set Matrix Zeroes
statement: If an element is 0, set its entire row and column to 0 (in place). Return the matrix.

title: Number of Islands
statement: Given a grid of '1' (land) and '0' (water), return the number of islands. An island is connected 4-directionally.

title: Max Area of Island
statement: Return the maximum area (number of cells) of an island (4-directionally connected 1s) in the grid, or 0 if none.

title: Flood Fill
statement: Perform a flood fill on the image starting at (sr, sc) with newColor, replacing the connected region of the same color (4-directionally). Return the image.
starter:
def flood_fill(image, sr, sc, color):
    pass

title: Count Cells With Both Neighbors Greater
statement: Given a matrix, return the count of interior cells (not on any border) whose value is strictly less than all four of its orthogonal neighbors.

title: Count Negative Numbers in a Sorted Matrix
statement: Given a matrix sorted in non-increasing order both row-wise and column-wise, return the count of negative numbers.
solution:
def count_negatives(grid):
    return sum(1 for row in grid for v in row if v<0)

title: Matrix Diagonal Sum
statement: Given a square matrix, return the sum of its two diagonals; the element shared by both diagonals (odd size) is counted once.

title: Course Schedule
statement: Given numCourses and prerequisites where [a,b] means you must take b before a, return True if you can finish all courses (no cycle).

title: Find the Town Judge
statement: In a town of n people, the judge trusts nobody and everybody (except the judge) trusts the judge. Given trust pairs [a,b] (a trusts b), return the judge's label or -1.
